title: dortmanna spicata
intro: Dortmanna spicata, also known as Sweet Woodruff, is a perennial herb native to Europe and North America, characterized by its small white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antispasmodic and a carminative, helping to relieve digestive issues and muscle spasms. It is also valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and acting as a natural diuretic, making it a popular remedy for urinary issues and fluid retention. Sweet Woodruff can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine and natural health practices.
